steps.title: Separate the sentence
steps.content: The first part says the Flying Cloud entered San Francisco Bay; that is a complete clause. The next sentence begins with a participial phrase about having left New York Harbor and then gives the main clause about setting a record.

steps.title: Reject comma-splice options
steps.content: Choice A joins sentence parts with and having in an unbalanced structure. Choice C creates a comma splice before the participial phrase. Choice D gives no punctuation after Bay, leaving the sentence fused. The answer is B.
